findings.summary: Accelerometer wearable with MPU-6050, XIAO ESP32C3, microSD card, and resistor pack pull-ups. I2C detected but SPI for SD card missed.

correct.description: MPU-6050 correctly identified as motion sensor IC
XIAO-ESP32C3 module correctly identified as IC
I2C bus between MPU-6050 and ESP32C3 correctly detected on SDA/SCL nets
R_Pack04 resistor networks (4.7k) correctly classified as resistor_network type
Battery correctly classified, +3V3 and GND power rails detected

incorrect.description: I2C pull-ups reported as has_pull_up: false, but RN1/RN2 resistor packs have pins connected to +3V3 rail. The 4.7k pull-ups are present but not connected to the I2C SDA/SCL nets directly - they pull up the SD card signals instead.

missed.description: MicroSD card (X1, Adafruit SDIO microSD symbol) uses SPI mode with CS, SCLK, DI (MOSI), DO (MISO) through RN1 series resistors to ESP32C3 but no SPI bus detected
RN1 provides series protection resistors on SD card SPI lines (CS, DI, SCLK via pins 7,6,5) with pull-ups to +3V3 (pins 1-4). This level-shifting/protection topology is not noted.

suggestions: SPI bus detection should recognize microSD card SPI signals even when routed through resistor networks
The microSD connector typed as connector is reasonable but could note SD card interface as a design observation
